reviews.headline: Three rounds, then radio silence for two months.
reviews.body: Recruiter set up final-round interviews with five panelists. Strong feedback in the moment. Then 8 weeks of nothing. No decline, no follow-up. Found out the role was filled internally on LinkedIn.

reviews.headline: Application tracker still says 'In review' six months later.
reviews.body: Two technical screens, one case study, then nothing. Their portal still shows the role open. No human on the other end of any email.

reviews.headline: First HR team in years that actually closed the loop.
reviews.body: Got a thoughtful rejection email with specific feedback within 48 hours of the final round. They asked permission to keep me in their pipeline. Restored some faith.

reviews.headline: Bait-and-switch on comp at the offer stage.
reviews.body: Verbal offer was 18% higher than the written one that arrived three days later. Recruiter blamed 'finance recalibration' and would not budge.

reviews.headline: Small team, but they treat candidates like adults.
reviews.body: Clear timeline, written process, and the founder showed up for the final conversation. Comp was on the screen, no negotiation games.

reviews.headline: Five rounds for a role that was already filled internally.
reviews.body: Found the internal hire announced before my final round. They still made me do the take-home.

reviews.headline: Four rounds, unpaid case study, then a form rejection.
reviews.body: They asked for a 40-hour deliverable framed as a 'take-home assessment.' When I asked to discuss scope, silence. Rejection came via automated email two weeks later with no feedback.

reviews.headline: Replied within 24 hours at every stage. Rare.
reviews.body: Three rounds, each with a clear agenda sent the day before. Recruiter followed up the same day after each interview. Even the rejection was delivered by phone with context. I'd interview with them again.

reviews.headline: Offer letter had a different title than what was discussed.
reviews.body: Three months of conversations about a Director-level role. Offer came in as Senior Manager with a 22% pay cut from what was discussed. They acted surprised I pushed back.

reviews.headline: Structured, respectful, and genuinely curious about fit.
reviews.body: Two rounds with clear rubrics shared upfront. Interviewers had read my CV. Got specific written feedback after declining their offer. Sets a standard.

reviews.headline: Interviewer asked if I planned to have children.
reviews.body: Second-round call with the hiring manager. Within ten minutes the conversation turned to my family plans and whether I had a spouse who could 'hold things down' if travel was required. I ended the call.

reviews.headline: Every email answered. Every deadline met on their side.
reviews.body: I sent three follow-up emails over the process and got a substantive response to each within a business day. Offer timeline matched what they said. Nothing exceptional, but consistent. And that counts.

reviews.headline: HR team seems completely disconnected from the hiring managers.
reviews.body: Recruiter told me the role had a Q2 start. Hiring manager said they weren't backfilling until Q4 at earliest. Three rounds of interviews with no alignment on what the role actually was.

reviews.headline: Exploding offer with a 48-hour window. No room to negotiate.
reviews.body: Got the written offer on a Friday at 4pm. Deadline was Sunday. Recruiter said extensions weren't possible. I asked for one week and they rescinded entirely.

reviews.headline: Took-home was scoped, time-boxed, and actually reviewed.
reviews.body: They sent a take-home with a strict two-hour limit and a note saying they wouldn't penalize for incomplete solutions. They wanted to see thinking, not polish. Debrief called out specific lines from my submission.
